svg.text: SERENITY-CLASS TILTROTOR — PHASED BUILD PLAN (REV J)
© 2026 Steve Griffing PE(CSE) CISSP-ISSEP CPP · CC BY 4.0
9 phases · first flight at Phase 3 (~$670) · full performance at Phase 7 (~$1,553 total) · install ALL conduits/voids/mounts in Phase 1 before foam pour
PHASE 0
PRINT ALL PARTS + CUT CF
• Dry filament 6h @ 65°C · install hardened steel nozzle
• Print ALL hull sections (PETG 8% gyroid 0.20mm)
• Print BOTH nacelle pods: 80mm ID (Phase 2) + 83mm ID (Phase 7)
• Print all nozzle parts, tilt brackets, cargo gondola, ToF mounts
• Print access-panel frames ×6 + lids ×6 (100% infill 2mm wall)
• Print TPU skids, cargo cradle, clamshell door halves
• Resin-print or source M0.5 bevel/pinion/crown gear set
• Cut CF keel 457.2mm · spars 2×300mm · ring frames · pivot rods
• Dry-fit: keel threads all hull sections · pod ID calipered vs EDF OD
TEST: dry-fit all panels · pod OD vs EDF · nozzle gear mesh
Cost: ~$65 (filament + CF + gears) | Cumulative: ~$65
PHASE 1
STRUCTURE + ALL PROVISIONS
• Bond access-panel frames A–F into hull with 5-min epoxy
• Install M2.5 nylon standoffs in all 4 node bays (A, B, D, E)
• Bond 12× ToF sensor PETG flush-mounts into hull cutouts
• Install M3 cargo hard-point threaded inserts (×4) belly
• Install SMA bulkheads: belly (SiK 915MHz) + dorsal (RCRS)
• Drill GPS coax hole at cockpit roof 59mm from nose
• Feed 6× PTFE conduits + pull strings nose-to-tail (label both ends)
• Insert waxed EPS void formers A–E · epoxy hull to keel · cure 12h
• FOAM POUR X-30 (≤60mL batches, 24h cure) · remove formers · bond caps
⚠ POINT OF NO RETURN — verify ALL provisions before foam pour
Cost: ~$80 (foam, epoxy, conduits, hardware) | Cumul: ~$145
PHASE 2
NACELLE MECH + BUDGET EDFs
• Press 4× MR63ZZ bearings flush ±0.2mm into nacelle pivot sockets
• Thread 8mm CF pivot rod · set screw + Loctite 243
• Install 2× MG90S tilt servos · 140° bracket (hard stop) · 18mm horn
• Install budget 80mm EDFs in nacelle_pod_80mm.stl · cap motor leads
• Install XFLY X4 PRO 40mm (final fuselage EDF) in engine bell
• Solder WS2812C nav LEDs: PORT=RED · STBD=GREEN · epoxy seal
• Assemble gear nozzle trains ×3 (bevel→shaft→crown→ring) · SG90 servos
• Nozzle sweep open=42mm / closed=36mm (verify with calipers)
• Verify blade clearance at 0°, 90°, 115° nacelle angles
TEST: sweep ×10 each · nozzle 42/36mm · LED polarity · 140° hard stop
Cost: ~$145 (budget EDFs + XFLY + servos) | Cumul: ~$290
PHASE 3
MINIMUM VIABLE FLYER
• 2× budget 80A BLHeli32 ESC + 1× BLHeli32 40A · XT90 PDB · 5V BEC
• Node 1: CM4 Lite + CM4-CARRIER-2 + SENSORHAT-1 + COMPHAT-SWITCH
• SENSORHAT-1 carries XIAO RP2350 real-time flight co-processor
• SiK 915MHz radio (on COMPHAT-SWITCH) + 49MHz RCRS-49 radio
• u-blox M10Q GPS · nav lights chain GP26 · 6S 4000mAh battery
• CPLD write-blocker flash · STM32 OTP fuses burn · TPM provision
• Flash XIAO RP2350 firmware · boot CM4 · install mavlink-router
• CG at 190mm · ESC calibration · servo calibration · radio check
• FAA reg N-number on airframe · ABCD pre-flight checklist
★ FIRST FLIGHT: tethered hover → free hover → transition ≥26ft AGL
Cost: ~$380 (Node 1 + ESCs + power + radios) | Cumul: ~$670
PHASE 4
NODE 4: ACTUATOR REDUNDANCY
• CM4 Lite + CM4-CARRIER-2 + SENSORHAT-1 + MICROHAT in bay E
• XIAO RP2350 on SENSORHAT-1 = dedicated real-time actuator ctrl
• CPLD write-blocker flash + STM32 OTP (separate from Node 1)
• N4 monitors N1 heartbeat via CAN FD; assumes control <100ms on N1 timeout
• N1→N4 CAN FD daisy-chain; add 120Ω termination at N4 end
• Pull CAN/RS-485/1553/ETH cables from conduits to bay E connectors
• N4 cross-checks IMU against N1 (agree ±2°/s on all axes)
• Provision Node 4 TPM independently (unique key material)
TEST: kill N1 → N4 holds hover within 100ms · IMU cross-check
Cost: ~$86 (CM4 + CARRIER-2 + SENSORHAT + MICROHAT) | Cumul: ~$756
PHASE 5
NODES 2 & 3 + FULL OA
• Node 2 (bay B): CM3+ Lite + CM3-CARRIER-1 → nav + waypoints
• Node 3 (bay D): CM3+ Lite + CM3-CARRIER-1 → payload + sensor
• Install OA Array B (×6 VL53L5CX, Node 2): S1B–S6B omnidirectional
• Install OA Array A (×6 VL53L5CX, Node 3): S1A–S6A spatially offset
• 12 sensors total · dual I2C buses · TCA9548A mux + MCP23008 XSHUT
• Full 4-node CAN FD ring · RS-485 multidrop · 1553 BC+RTs · ETH star
• Autonomous 3-waypoint mission · GPS-guided · RTL on link loss
• OA halt at 1.0m · dual-array fusion 15Hz · single node OA coverage OK
TEST: all 12 ToF valid · wall halt 1.0m · 3-wpt autonomous mission
Cost: ~$177 (2× CM3+ + CM3-CARRIER + 12× ToF) | Cumul: ~$933
PHASE 6
CARGO SYSTEM
• Epoxy cargo gondola shell into belly void at M3 hard points
• Gondola: 101.6×76.2×76.2mm interior · 18mm belly protrusion
• Install clamshell door halves on 3mm CF hinge pins
• N20 300:1 winch motor + drum · 1.5m Dyneema SK75 0.5mm
• DRV8833 H-bridge driver · SG90 door servo · SG90 release servo
• Auto-latch cradle on Dyneema: no manual loading in flight
• Node 3 GPIO → cargo commands · seal gondola-hull joint
TEST: 250g load ×5 · winch deploy/retract · T/W ≥2.60 with cargo
Cost: ~$30 (winch + servos + Dyneema) | Cumul: ~$963
PHASE 7
MOTOR UPGRADE: XRP + 120A ESC
• Swap nacelle_pod_80mm → nacelle_pod_83mm (external pod — no hull work)
• Install 2× Changesun XRP 3660-2700KV 80mm 6S: CCW (port) + CW (stbd)
• XRP draws 84A per nacelle — MANDATORY 120A ESC. Do not reuse 80A.
• Replace 2× budget ESC → 2× Hobbywing Platinum PRO V4 120A
• Recalibrate Hobbywing ESCs per Hobbywing arm procedure
• Recheck CG: XRP pair +766g delta vs budget pair → shift battery fwd
• Bench thrust: ≥2,900g/nacelle · ≥6,450g total · ESC ≤80°C
• Incremental payload test: 500g → 750g → 1,000g (max 1,046g at T/W 2.0)
★ FULL PERF: T/W 2.81 empty · 2.66 with cargo · 1,046g max payload
Cost: ~$590 (2× XRP $340 + 2× Hobbywing 120A $250) | Cumul: ~$1,553
PHASE 8
FINISHING + FAA + DOCS
• Replace N00000 placeholder with issued FAA Part 48 N-number
• Print waterslide decal sheet (seal with clear coat after printing)
• Apply decals per build_guide_19_decal_placement.svg:
A: SERENITY lettering · B: FAA reg (REQUIRED) · C: universe markings
D: safety labels (sUAS data plate, LiPo, limits) · E: weathering
• Final airworthiness inspection: all fasteners · CG · antenna secure
• Archive firmware checksums, build log, test records
• FAA compliance: N-number visible · Part 107 cert current · <55 lb AUW
ABCD: Airframe · Battery · Comms · Docs — all green before each flight
Cost: ~$20 (decal paper + printing) | TOTAL: ~$1,573
First flight at Phase 3: ~$670 total invested | Full build end-state: ~$1,573 | All conduits/voids installed Phase 1 — no structural rework after foam pour
Hull: Peter Farell CC BY 4.0 · Nozzle: BamJr CC BY 4.0 · Visual inspiration: Firefly/Serenity © Joss Whedon/Mutant Enemy/Universal — Fan engineering work
